generated_oml:
instance LightSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Light sensor integrated into the building envelope reports illumination levels."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightSensorTelemetry
]

instance TemperatureHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature and humidity sensor integrated into the building envelope reports ambient temperature and humidity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TempHumidityTelemetry
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy detector integrated into the building envelope reports presence of occupants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyTelemetry
]

instance LightSensorTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightSensor
]

instance TempHumidityTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureHumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance LightData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Telemetry data for illumination received via Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightSensorTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance TempHumidityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Telemetry data of temperature and humidity received via Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TempHumidityTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Telemetry data of occupancy status received via Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
]

instance LightFixtureActuator : DTDFVocab:DTComponent [
    base:desc "Controllable light fixture that receives brightness and color temperature commands via Ditto."
]

instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:DTComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC component that receives setpoint commands via Ditto."
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-style at‑least‑once messaging middleware for transmitting sensor telemetry and receiving actuator commands."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, RealTimeControlService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Persistent device registry, event store, and policy engine for storing telemetry and routing commands."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, RealTimeControlService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseBasyx : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "OPC UA-based digital twin data exchange implementation for delivering twin models and state."
    DTDFVocab:enables BasyxTwinExchangeService
]

instance iTwinJS : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Infrastructure-specific web API for interacting with building digital twins via iTwin."
    DTDFVocab:enables iTwinAccessService
]

instance UE5Visualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 visualization engine rendering the digital twin with immersive interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboardService
]

instance MDEPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model‑Driven Engineering pipeline that validates enriched BIM models and extracts device telemetry structure."
    DTDFVocab:enables DittoDeviceExtractionService
]

instance EnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM model exported from Revit containing device metadata and control parameters."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEPipeline
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of building operating conditions via continuous data exchange."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance RealTimeControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time control of HVAC and lighting actuators based on building control logic."
    DTDFVocab:provides LightControlAction, HVACControlAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics for maintenance and optimization of energy consumption."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenancePredictionInsight
]

instance VisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization dashboards that provide immersive interfaces for Facility Managers and Building Control Engineers."
    DTDFVocab:provides InteractiveVisualizationInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current state of temperature, occupancy, and energy usage from real‑time telemetry."
]

instance MaintenancePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Maintenance predictions for building components based on historical telemetry."
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive visual representation of building state rendered in UE5."
]

instance LightControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to set lighting brightness and color temperature via Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance HVACControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to control HVAC setpoint via Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance LivingLabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LightSensor, TemperatureHumiditySensor, OccupancySensor,
                 LightFixtureActuator, HVACActuator,
                 EclipseHono, EclipseDitto, EclipseBasyx, iTwinJS, UE5Visualization, MDEPipeline
]

instance BasyxTwinExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for exchanging twin data via Eclipse Basyx."
]

instance iTwinAccessService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for accessing building digital twins via iTwin.js."
]

instance DittoDeviceExtractionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for extracting Ditto device representations from enriched BIM models."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT framework supports command flows from the digital twin back to the physical building, allowing facility managers to affect building control systems. While the framework enables delegate of control commands from the twin to the physical device layer, the exact command types, message formats, validation and authentication mechanisms, feedback loops, conflict resolution strategies, and execution confirmation details are not explicitly described in the documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Real‑time continuous synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin is achieved via continuous data exchange. Update cycles are implicitly driven by the intrinsic sampling intervals of sensors and actuators in the physical system; the framework does not specify explicit latency thresholds but ensures sub‑real‑time responsiveness to changes in the physical environment."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The orchestration follows a model‑driven engineering pipeline: enriched BIM models containing device, sensor, actuator, and controller definitions are extracted using MDE principles into a structured representation (e.g., XML/JSON). This representation is automatically transformed into deployable runtime artifacts—services, data stores, visualization components—managed by the central DT framework. The framework provides a modular architecture with standardized extension points, enabling integration of custom services and analytics. Resource management is handled by runtime configuration management, allowing adjustments without full redeployment. System‑wide coordination is achieved through bidirectional data exchange channels and a unified interface for stakeholders."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "Development begins with Building Control Engineers annotating an enriched BIM model to define sensors, actuators, and control logic. Using Model‑Driven Engineering (MDE) the framework extracts a structured, semantic representation and automatically generates deployable Digital Twin artefacts (R2). A runtime deployment process, powered by Eclipse Hono (for MQTT‑based message brokering) and Eclipse Ditto (for twin representation), synchronises live data between the physical building and the digital model. The Operational phase is overseen by Facility Managers, who interact with an interactive visualisation interface to monitor states and issue control commands. Transition management is facilitated by a modular architecture with defined, standardized extension points (R4), ensuring reproducible deployments and lifecycle governance aligned with the roles described in Table I."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model validity is enforced at multiple stages of the deployment pipeline. During the model‑checking (5.53 s) and model‑parsing (8.7 s) phases, enriched BIM models are validated against semantic constraints defined in the framework (e.g., proper linkage between devices and controllers). Conversion and configuration steps transform these models into Ditto entities while preserving relationships and ensuring that device metadata conforms to Ditto policy schemas. Policy‑based validation within Ditto enforces value ranges and units for sensor data, and pre‑execution checks for actuator commands prevent conflicts. Fault tolerance is achieved via Eclipse Hono’s at‑least‑once delivery and Ditto’s persistent event store, allowing retransmission of lost or delayed messages. The overall entanglement quality was quantified using the ODTE metric: a timeliness score of 0.997, reliability of 1.0, and availability of 1.0, aggregating to a total ODTE of 0.997. This indicates that the twin’s state is essentially indistinguishable from the physical counterpart under the tested conditions, confirming high fidelity and validating the model‑data synchronization. While no explicit uncertainty quantification method is documented, the validation and detection mechanisms (policy checks, at‑least‑once guarantee, persistent storage) provide robust error detection and correction at run‑time."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The framework’s technical network infrastructure relies on Eclipse Hono and Eclipse Ditto for real‑time connectivity, facilitating bidirectional exchange between the digital twin and physical assets. Extension points allow integration of additional services, but the documents do not provide explicit details on network topology, bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security protocols beyond the use of these middleware components."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "No explicit deployment platform, such as cloud, edge, or on‑premise hosting, is identified in the documents. The framework is described as modular with runtime configuration management and automated deployment, but concrete computational or storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, supported deployment models, or availability assurances are not specified."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to industry BIM standards for the initial design model and leverages open‑source DT stacks—Eclipse Ditto and BaSyx—which comply with MQTT and JSON‑based API specifications. Extension points are defined following standard software‑component interface patterns, facilitating interoperability across AECO systems. This enables compliance with broader IEEE and IFC interoperability guidelines although explicit certification is not cited in the documents."
]
